_doc: OPERATOR presentation overlay (commerce + presentation, operator-owned). The soverella emitter MERGES the machine-made engine (content + routing) with this overlay to produce engine.json in the runtime's terminals schema. Bee-D never sets tier/severity. Dishes take heading from the menu label and result_copy from the grounded result_state unless overridden here. Escapes REQUIRE heading + result_copy here (the engine carries none). tier=67 is FORCED on every escape by the translator regardless of this file. SEED map (old terminal -> new state) is a PROPOSAL for operator approval at the diff HOLD.
_seed_map_proposal: LANE E — corrected dish set (6q/23opt/2esc/7dishes). Old IDs were renamed by the corrected pipeline; high_complexity ESCAPE is retired and its premium/critical intent is absorbed by the grounded dasp-multiple-funds $147 dish. Tiers/severities preserved from the old seed where the concept persists; the two 147 assignments (unclaimed-super-transfer kept from old timing-transfer-to-ato; multiple-funds new per directive) are flagged for approval.

dasp-eligibility-check._seed_from: old dasp-eligibility-check (kept; now the q1 unsure_eligibility determination terminal)

dasp-unclaimed-super-transfer._seed_from: old dasp-timing-transfer-to-ato (renamed; 6-month ATO transfer; 147 confirmed; severity AMBER per graph-corrections standing ruling — money not lost, route changes)

dasp-multiple-funds._seed_from: NEW — grounded mixed-visa / multi-fund $147 dish; absorbs retired high_complexity escape's premium+critical intent (approval: 147 per directive; severity red inherited from high_complexity, could be amber)

none_fit.heading: DASP may not apply to your situation
none_fit.result_copy: Your answers suggest DASP may not fit — for example you are an Australian or New Zealand citizen or permanent resident, or you held an excluded visa subclass (405 or 410), so the ordinary Australian super rules apply instead. A short personalised review works through what does apply to your circumstances and the steps to take next.

insufficient_information.heading: Your situation needs a closer look
insufficient_information.result_copy: Your answers do not yet confirm which DASP position applies to you — a key detail (such as your visa type, how long since you left, or your balance) is unconfirmed. A short personalised review confirms where you stand and exactly what to check before you claim.
